refined_fr.refined_fr: Ensure the teleoperation link availability meets or exceeds the customer-defined target to maintain continuous remote control capability.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly focus on link availability as a measurable performance metric critical for teleoperation effectiveness and safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target (≥ 0.999).
sub_frs: Maintain continuous network link connectivity with minimal downtime.
Detect and recover from link failures promptly to restore teleoperation control.
Monitor link quality and report status to teleoperation control systems.

candidate_dps: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms
Real-time link health monitoring and automatic recovery protocols
Adaptive bandwidth management and error correction algorithms
selected_dp.selected_dp: Redundant network link architecture with failover mechanisms

potential_sources: Dependency on network infrastructure and teleoperation control systems
Interaction with safety services for alerting on link failures
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates link availability management and supports modular failover without tight coupling to other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of redundant network paths and infrastructure.
Risk of increased system complexity leading to potential integration issues.
Assumes failover mechanisms do not introduce unacceptable latency.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring DP in next decomposition level to enhance recovery speed.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation link connectivity, failover, and status reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections used for teleoperation data transmission and failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report link status and health metrics to the NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Interface to notify Safety Services of link failures or degraded availability impacting safety.

module_containment_scores.notes: Contains overall system but link availability logic is diffused; low containment for this DP.
Vehicle module depends on link but does not contain link management; poor containment.
High containment of link availability DP; low diffusion risk as logic is centralized.
Receives alerts but does not contain link availability logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management in the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Interface dependencies on Network Link and SafetyAlert must be robust to avoid cascading failures.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface robustness and consider backup mechanisms within Teleoperation module.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear measurable criteria and logical solution approach.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is well contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and safety alert logs is feasible and traceable to the IM.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility and interfaces to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Enhance monitoring and recovery DP in next level decomposition.
Strengthen interface error handling between Teleoperation and Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition and assignments are sound, further refinement of sub-FRs and DP details is needed to address recovery and monitoring explicitly.
next_level_sub_frs: Maintain continuous network link connectivity with minimal downtime.
Detect and recover from link failures promptly to restore teleoperation control.
Monitor link quality and report status to teleoperation control systems.

revise.why: To explicitly capture recovery and monitoring functions as separate FRs and corresponding DPs for improved clarity and design robustness.

assumptions: Teleoperation module can implement failover mechanisms effectively.
open_risks: Complexity of failover may impact latency and safety.
Interface robustness between modules needs validation.
evidence_needed: Performance data on failover latency and link availability.
Verification test results of interface error handling.
